narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads CSM at 100 on both D1 and H4 in a Risk-Off High regime, driven by rising inflation expectations pushing US 10Y yields to 4.595% while S&P 500 drops 1.24% and copper collapses 4.81%. USDCHF is the cleanest expression with D1+H4 Strong Buy and H4 momentum at 78, confirmed by EURUSD's -0.78 correlation amplifying USD strength; NZDUSD adds a second lane with H4 Strong Sell and NZDJPY correlation at 0.93 compressing any NZD relief. AUD H4 CSM at 26 versus D1 at 65 is a 39-point divergence flagged as the critical short-term signal — AUDUSD D1 Neutral clashes with H4 Sell and momentum fading hard, meaning AUD longs built on D1 bias are offside now.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: USDCAD CMP 60, D1 59 rising, H4 70 rising, both timeframes aligned higher
Fading: GBPUSD CMP 36, NZDUSD CMP 43, EURUSD CMP 37, H4 momentum at 13 across all three

WATCH
Oil sustains above 105.50 triggering USDCAD reversal pressure given CADJPY -0.71 correlation to USDCAD
Silver extends below 77.00 accelerating NZDUSD downside as commodity currency correlation breaks further
VIX breaks above 20.00 validating full risk-off and sending GBPJPY toward next support with H4 Sell intact

themes.theme: USD strength driven by inflation expectations and rising bond yields
Risk-off sentiment with equity selloff on inflation concerns
Commodity complex weakness across softs despite energy strength
Oil supply disruption concerns supporting energy complex
Brazilian Real weakness pressuring sugar prices

key_observation: Strong USD rally on inflation-driven bond yield surge coinciding with broad equity selloff. Commodity markets show divergence with energy supported by supply concerns while agricultural softs decline. Brazilian Real weakness adds pressure to sugar prices.
watch: Bond yield trajectory and Fed expectations. Equity market stabilization. Oil supply developments. USD momentum sustainability into next week. Real weakness continuation.
